name: Burrata with grilled grapes and basil
intro: the fridge for up to 1 day before grilling. If you can‚Äôt get hold of burrata, balls of

ingredients.item: oz/320g seedless red grapes, pulled off their vine
Valdespino vinegar (or other best-quality sherry vinegar)
fennel seeds, toasted and lightly crushed flaked sea salt and black pepper
large balls of burrata or buffalo mozzarella (1 lb 5 oz/600g)
small purple or green basil sprigs (¬º cup/5g), to serve

method: Put the grapes in a medium bowl with the vinegar, oil, garlic, sugar, 1 tsp of the fennel seeds, ¬º tsp of ÔÄÇ aked salt, and plenty of pepper. Mix well and marinate for at least 1 hour and up to 1 day. Thread 5 or 6 grapes onto each skewer. Don‚Äôt throw away the marinade; you‚Äôll need it when serving.
Place a grill pan over high heat and ventilate your kitchen well. Once hot, add the grape skewers in batches and grill for 2‚Äì3 minutes, turning after 1¬Ω minutes. Remove from the heat.
When ready to serve, tear the balls of burrata in half and place one-half on each plate. Arrange the grape skewers to lean against them‚Äî2 per portion

‚Äîand spoon 1¬Ω tsp of the marinade over the cheese. Alternatively, arrange on a platter to serve a crowd. Sprinkle with the remaining ¬Ω tsp of fennel seeds, garnish with a sprig of basil, and serve.

If you‚Äôre doubling or tripling this recipe, break the cauliÔÄÇower into ÔÄÇorets and pop them into a food processor (rather than grating by hand). Pulse a few times to blitz it up into tabbouleh-like pieces, but don‚Äôt overwork the machine, as the cauliÔÄÇower will turn to a mush. Toasted pistachios, roughly chopped, can be used instead of (or as well as) the pomegranate seeds, if you want a crunchy garnish.
